POST /accounts/{account_id}/workers/obse.description: Run a temporary or saved query.

chart.description: Whether to include timeseties data in the response

compare.description: Whether to include comparison data with previous time periods

dry.description: Whether to perform a dry run without saving the results of the query. Useful for validation

granularity.description: This is only used when the view is calculations. Leaving it empty lets Workers Observability detect the correct granularity.

ignoreSeries.description: Whether to ignore time-series data in the results and return only aggregated values

limit.description: Use this limit to cap the number of events returned when the view is events.

offset.description: Cursor pagination for event/trace/invocation views. Pass the last item's $metadata.id as the next offset.

offsetBy.description: Numeric offset for pattern results (top-N list). Use with limit to page pattern groups; not used by cursor pagination.

parameters.description: Optional parameters to pass to the query execution

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

key.description: The key to use for the calculation. This key must exist in the logs. Use the observability_keys response to confirm. Do not guess keys.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

anyOf.description: Filtering best practices: use observability_keys and observability_values to confirm available fields and values. If searching for errors, filter for $metadata.error exists.

key.description: Filter field name. IMPORTANT: do not guess keys. Always use verified keys from previous query results or the observability_keys response. Preferred keys: $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, $metadata.error.

value.description: Filter comparison value. IMPORTANT: must match actual values in your logs. Verify using previous query results or the /values endpoint. Ensure value type matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive unless using specific operations. Regex uses ClickHouse RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds); examples: ^5\d{2}$ for HTTP 5xx, \bERROR\b for word boundary.

items.description: Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

queryId.description: Unique identifier for the query to execute

timeframe.description: Timeframe for your query using Unix timestamps in milliseconds. Provide from/to epoch ms; narrower timeframes provide faster responses and more specific results.

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

view.description: Examples by view type. Events: show errors for a worker in the last 30 minutes. Calculations: p99 of wall time or count by status code. Invocations: find a specific request that resulted in a 500.

requestBody.description: Query your observability events, requests, and traces to build visualizations and identify insights.

items.description: The data structure of a telemetry event

$containers.description: Cloudflare Containers event information enriches your logs so you can easily identify and debug issues.

id.description: Unique event ID. Use as the cursor for offset-based pagination.

$workers.description: Cloudflare Workers event information enriches your logs so you can easily identify and debug issues.

items.description: The data structure of a telemetry event

$containers.description: Cloudflare Containers event information enriches your logs so you can easily identify and debug issues.

id.description: Unique event ID. Use as the cursor for offset-based pagination.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

abr_level.description: The level of Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) sampling used for the query. If empty the ABR level is 1

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.

timeframe.description: Time range for the query execution

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

statistics.description: The statistics object contains information about query performance from the database, it does not include any network latency

abr_level.description: The level of Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) sampling used for the query. If empty the ABR level is 1

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.
